title: Design an event-driven fan-out
description: Decompose a workload into a producer plus parallel consumers via a queue/topic, with idempotency and backpressure, instead of synchronous chaining.
body: Steps: 1. Identify the unit of work and whether order matters. 2. Have the producer publish events to a queue/topic rather than synchronously invoking consumers. 3. Make each consumer idempotent and independently retryable. 4. Apply backpressure: batch size, reserved concurrency, and a DLQ for failures. 5. Add tracing so a single logical request is followable across functions. Rollback point: before switching a synchronous path to async (changes the consistency model). Validation: load tests show consumers scale without throttling downstreams and duplicates cause one effect; synchronous fan-out that fans into concurrency limits is the anti-pattern this replaces.
